title: 7.7 practice
description: Practice what you learned in lesson 7.7!
topic_path: The Long Nineteenth Century (1750 to 1914 CE)

assessment_items.question_content:
**How did Evangelical Christianity contribute to social reform movements in the 19th century?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It inspired reformers to address moral and social problems like slavery, alcoholism, and poverty.
It encouraged believers to withdraw from public life and focus solely on personal salvation.
It led to the rejection of education and literacy in favor of spiritual experience.
It limited reform efforts to church communities and discouraged involvement in politics.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What reform was directly prompted by the 1911 Triangle Shirtwaist Factory fire in New York City?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: legislation requiring improved factory safety standards
legislation requiring increased wages for factory workers
legislation mandating longer and more frequent breaks for workers
legislation enforcing minimum age limits for factory workers

assessment_items.question_content:
>There would be meat that had tumbled out on the floor, in the dirt and sawdust, where the workers had tramped and spit uncounted billions of consumption germs. There would be meat stored in great piles in rooms; and the water from leaky roofs would drip over it, and thousands of rats would race about on it.

>—*The Jungle* by Upton Sinclair, 1906


**What was Upton Sinclair’s primary purpose in writing *The Jungle*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: To expose unsafe practices in meatpacking and demand reform.
To highlight the economic strength of U.S. industry.
To argue that American food was cleaner than European food.
To celebrate industrial jobs available to immigrants.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best identifies a key difference between mercantilist capitalism and industrial capitalism?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Mercantilism emphasized trade and state control, while industrial capitalism focused on factory production.
Mercantilism used machines and factories, while industrial capitalism used manual labor.
Mercantilism relied on colonies for wealth, while industrial capitalism relied on factories and wage labor.
Mercantilist capitalism supported free markets, while industrial capitalism relied on state control.

assessment_items.question_content:
**In what way can capitalism be understood as a *cultural system*, not just an economic one?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It shapes people’s values, behaviors, and identities around work, success, and consumption.
It operates the same way in every country and culture.
It encourages cultures to reject industrialization and economic growth.
It eliminates the need for governments to regulate social behavior.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Why did Karl Marx believe capitalism was not consistent with human nature?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It forced people to compete rather than work cooperatively and express their creativity.
It encouraged too much government control over people’s lives.
It promoted equality by removing class differences.
It allowed workers to freely choose their jobs and working conditions.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Karl Marx coined the term “proletariat” to describe a particular social class. Which group does this term refer to?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A class of people who sell their labor for wages
A class of people who own businesses and factories and earn profit from them
A class of people legally bound to work land owned by a noble
A class of people forced to work without pay or freedom

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is one way that industrial capitalism exploited the proletariat, according to critics like Karl Marx?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It allowed capitalists to control key resources.
It allowed the proletariat to control key resources.
It limited women’s opportunities for economic mobility.
It improved conditions for all laborers.

assessment_items.question_content:
**How did unions contribute to the success of labor movements during the industrial era?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They gave workers a way to organize collectively.
They focused on ending slavery.
They provided literacy education to workers.
They centered their efforts on women’s rights.

assessment_items.question_content:
Germany operates a mostly capitalist economy with private businesses and a strong global market presence. However, the government provides universal healthcare that is funded through taxes, ensuring all citizens have access to medical services regardless of income.


**Which statement best explains how Germany combines socialist principles with capitalism?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The government provides certain public services to ensure everyone has equal access.
Germany discourages tax-funded programs and relies on charities for social welfare.
Germany relies entirely on government-run industries instead of private companies.
The government controls all industries and limits private business activities.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following photograph was taken by Jacob Riis in the late 19th century. 

[[☃ image 1]]

**What was Jacob Riis’s primary purpose in publishing photographs like this one?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A black-and-white photograph showing a cramped tenement room crowded with several people. Some individuals are lying on the floor under blankets while others sit on bunk beds against the wall. The space is cluttered with belongings, cooking pots, and a small stove, illustrating the poor living conditions faced by working-class families and immigrants in urban areas during the late 19th century.
options.caption: "Lodgers in a Crowded Bayard Street Tenement-'Five Cents a Spot'" By Jacob Riis, public domain.

choices.content: To expose the harsh living conditions in tenements and push for housing reform.
To celebrate the cultural diversity of American cities.
To sell photographs of immigrant life to art collectors and newspapers.
To promote immigration by showing that even the poorest Americans had housing.

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]]

**What message does this 1917 political cartoon convey about labor unions during the labor movement?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: The black-and-white cartoon shows a large group of workers—men holding tools like hammers, shovels, and pitchforks—marching together in unity. As they join, their bodies form a massive, raised clenched fist emerging from the ground. In the background are industrial factories and smokestacks, suggesting the workers' struggle in an industrialized society.
options.caption: “Solidarity“ By Ralph Chaplin (1917), public domain.
options.title: “The Hand That Will Rule the World—One Big Union.”

choices.content: Workers gained power by uniting through unions to demand better wages and conditions.
Labor unions weakened industry by encouraging workers to stop working.
Only individual workers could negotiate successfully with factory owners.
Unions were only useful in rural, agricultural areas.
